Rizzuto crime family.description: Italian Canadian crime family based in Montreal, Quebec, founded in the 1970s by Nicolo Rizzuto. It operates primarily in southern Quebec and Ontario, involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, illegal gambling, loan sharking, extortion, weapons trafficking, and murder. The FBI considers it a faction of the Bonanno crime family, while Canadian authorities view it as independent. After internal power struggles and deaths of key members, the family is currently led by Leonardo Rizzuto.

Gambino crime family.description: The Gambino family is one of the Five Families of New York City and one of the most powerful mafia organizations in the United States. Originating from the Black Hand gang led by Ignazio Lupo and Giuseppe Morello in East Harlem, it evolved under bosses such as Salvatore D'Aquila, Al Mineo, and Vincent Mangano. The family was formally established as one of the Five Families by Salvatore Maranzano in 1931. It remains active in organized crime activities including illegal gambling and racketeering.

The raid targeted the Gambino and Bonanno families in the U.S. while in Italy, it targeted the Ursino 'ndrina from Gioiosa Jonica.

edges.evidence_quote: By 1987, the relationship between the two families had deteriorated. Gotti had sent word to Manna that the Genovese family should take over Bruno's North Jersey territory and leave more profitable South Jersey operations in Philadelphia and Atlantic City to the Gambinos. Manna, enraged and insulted over the offer, began planning John and Gene Gotti's assassinations.

descriptions: The Genovese family had territory tension with the Gambino family over rackets in Hartford.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2024, longtime West Springfield mobster Frank "Frankie Pugs" Pugliano helped the Genovese family end territory tension after Gambino mobster Carmine "Carm the Barber" Manzi, also based in Springfield, began expanding rackets into the Hartford area of Connecticut.

Ejército Revolucionario Popular Antisubv.description: Colombian narcoparamilitary organization and one of the emerging criminal gangs serving drug trafficking (Bacrim), part of the Colombian internal armed conflict. Founded by Pedro Oliviero Guerrero Castillo alias 'Cuchillo' after demobilizing from the Bloque Héroes del Guaviare, with 200 men. It was the heir of the Bloque Centauros of the AUC and allied with drug trafficker Daniel Barrera. After the death of Cuchillo and capture of Barrera, 500 members surrendered in 2011, and the group split into two factions.

Roach Guards.description: The Roach Guards were an ethnically Irish criminal gang in the Five Points neighborhood of New York City in the early to mid-19th century. Founded by Ted Roach, the gang originally protected liquor merchants before engaging in robbery and murder. They fought rival gangs such as the Bowery Boys and declined during the 1850s, disappearing by 1865.

Omega.description: Omega is a prison gang turned organized crime group based in Singapore, formed in 1989 in Chia Keng Prison by Yan Bai. It is a monoethnic Malay Muslim gang that emerged to protect Malay inmates from discrimination by Chinese-dominated secret societies. The group evolved into a criminal enterprise involved in illicit drug trade and other activities, with networks extending beyond Singapore.

descriptions: Omega recruited members from the Malay Muslim factions of Sio Kun Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: At its inception in 1989, Omega mainly enlisted independent Malay Muslim inmates that were not in any gang, members from pre-existing small-scale Malay groups, as well as members from the Malay Muslim factions of Chinese secret societies, such as Sio Kun Tong, also known as Salakau or 369.

descriptions: Salakau routinely attacked Omega.
edges.evidence_quote: Attacks on rival gangs such as Sakongsa, Omega and 18 Sio Yi Ho were somewhat of a routine occurrence.

Martinez Familia Sangeros.description: A criminal organization based in Puerto Rico, founded in the 1980s by Quitoni Martinez. It consists of six crime families, with the Estrella Family being the primary one. The group is involved in drug trafficking, loan sharking, extortion, and other crimes, with connections to Colombian cartels and international operations. It remains active with an estimated 1200-1500 members.
